title: Meet the NH online influencer who lost 300 lbs — and isn’t done yet
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News “I made a promise to myself that I was going to take my life back,” Salem, Mass.
native Caitlin Harrington says.
Caitlin Harrington before losing 600 pounds (left) next to a photo of her after losing half her body weight (right).

title: Mass. man charged again with impersonating federal officer in immigration scam
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime Francisco Soares, of Foxborough, took thousands of dollars from victims during alleged scam, authorities say.
A Foxborough man is facing new charges for allegedly posing as a federal immigration officer to defraud immigrants, authorities said.
Francisco Soares, 56, was arrested April 30, according to a statement from Massachusetts U.

title: Wrong-way driver in crash that killed Mass. state trooper identified
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News In the wake of the crash, a fundraiser has been started for the family of State Trooper Kevin Trainor.

State Police troopers, firefighters and police officers bowed their heads after the motorcade carrying fallen State Police Trooper Kevin Trainor passed by en route to the medical examiner’s office in Boston on Wednesday.

Jessica Rinaldi/Globe Staff Previously: Authorities have identified the wrong-way driver who struck a Massachusetts State Trooper’s cruiser on Route 1 in Lynnfield early Wednesday, killing State Trooper Kevin Trainor.

title: Quincy argues in front of SJC to install 10-foot patron saint statues at public safety building
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News “In this country, public art doesn’t become off-limits just because it may make some people think about religion.
" Models of statues that are proposed to be situated outside of the new public safety headquarters in Quincy.

(Office of Mayor Thomas Koch) The state’s highest court heard oral arguments Wednesday in the City of Quincy’s appeal to install two 10-foot, bronze statues of Catholic patron saints in front of the city’s new police and fire building.

title: Jaylen Brown pushes back against report of ‘frustration’ with Celtics: ‘I love Boston’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics "If it was up to me, I’d play in Boston for the next 10 years.
I’ll say it again: I hate that Brad even had to respond to this.
” Jaylen Brown took to Twitch again on Wednesday to clarify some of his comments.

title: Sonny Gray shuts down Tigers in return from injury, Red Sox finish sweep with 4-0 win
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Boston Red Sox Gray (3-1) was making his first start since facing the Tigers on April 20 at Fenway Park — a game he left after 2 2/3 innings with a strained hamstring.
Red Sox pitcher Sonny Gray throws against the Detroit Tigers during the first inning.

AP Photo/Paul Sancya DETROIT (AP) — Sonny Gray pitched five innings in his return from injury and the Boston Red Sox finished a three-game sweep of the Detroit Tigers with a 4-0 win on Wednesday night.

title: Man found guilty on firearm charges connected to 2023 Caribbean festival shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The shooting, which happened near the festival’s J’ouvert Parade, took place early in the morning and injured eight people.
Gerald Vick is arraigned after the J'ouvert parade shooting in August 2023.
David L.

title: Fourth State Police academy staffer pleads not guilty to manslaughter in trainee’s death
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The trooper is the fourth staffer to be arraigned for manslaughter after a recruit died from injuries sustained during a training exercise.
State Police recruit Enrique Delgado-Garcia died in 2024 from injuries sustained during a police academy training exercise.
Worcester County DA's Office A fourth State Police academy staffer charged in the 2024 death of trainee Enrique Delgado-Garcia was arraigned on Wednesday.

title: Symphony MBTA station will be closed for 3 years amid accessibility renovations
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Transportation “When finished, Symphony Station will be a bright, modernized, and fully accessible station that all can be proud of for generations to come,” Phil Eng, general manager of the MBTA and the state’s interim transportation secretary, said.
Symphony Station along the E branch of the Green Line.
